prerequisites: A file reachable over a supported storage provider: HTTPS (public web), Google Cloud Storage (GCS), Amazon S3, Azure Blob Storage (AzBlob), SSH/SCP/SFTP, or a local filesystem path.
The full URL path to the file to replicate (the `url` config field), e.g. an https:// link, gs:// or s3:// path, or an SFTP path.
The file `format` (one of csv, json, jsonl, excel, excel_binary, fwf, feather, parquet, yaml).
A `dataset_name` to use as the destination table name (letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores only).

Provider-specific credentials when the file is private: a GCS service account JSON, AWS access key id + secret access key for S3, an Azure storage account name plus a SAS token or shared key, or a host/user/password for SSH/SCP/SFTP. Credentials are not required for public files.

authMethods.name: GCS service account JSON (provider.service_account_json)
authMethods.summary: For private Google Cloud Storage buckets, supply the JSON key of a Google service account that has read access to the file. Omit it for public GCS data.

authMethods.name: AWS access key (provider.aws_secret_access_key)
authMethods.summary: For private Amazon S3 buckets, provide an AWS access key id and the matching secret access key with read permission on the object. Omit both for public S3 data.

authMethods.name: Azure Blob SAS token or shared key (provider.sas_token / provider.shared_key)
authMethods.summary: For Azure Blob Storage, set the storage account name and authenticate with either a SAS token or the account shared key. Credentials are optional for public blobs.

authMethods.name: SSH/SCP/SFTP password (provider.password)
authMethods.summary: For SSH, SCP, or SFTP providers, supply the host, user, and an optional password (port defaults to 22) to fetch the remote file.

setupSteps.title: Choose the storage provider
setupSteps.body: Set the `provider` config to where the file lives: HTTPS, GCS, S3, AzBlob, SSH, SCP, SFTP, or local filesystem. The provider determines which credentials (if any) are required.

setupSteps.title: Point the connector at the file
setupSteps.body: Set `url` to the file's path or URL and choose the `format` (csv, json, jsonl, excel, excel_binary, fwf, feather, parquet, or yaml). Set `dataset_name` to the destination table name using only letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials for private files
setupSteps.body: If the file is private, add the provider's secret fields: GCS `service_account_json`; S3 `aws_access_key_id` + `aws_secret_access_key`; Azure `storage_account` plus `sas_token` or `shared_key`; or SSH/SCP/SFTP `host`, `user`, and `password`. Leave credentials empty for publicly accessible files.

setupSteps.title: Tune parsing with reader options (optional)
setupSteps.body: Optionally set `reader_options` to a JSON string of pandas reader arguments for the chosen format (for example read_csv options for CSV or read_json options for JSON) to control how the file is parsed.

sources.title: File (CSV, JSON, Excel, Feather, Parquet) Connector - Airbyte Docs
